title: Ceropegia leachii
taxonomy: Ceropegia leachii is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to India and is a member of the genus Ceropegia. The genus Ceropegia is characterized by its unique, twisted flowers that resemble the shape of a snake's hood. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Ceropegia leachii shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as the presence of milky sap. The classification of Ceropegia leachii reflects its unique appearance and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
